topic: OpenTofu vs Terraform Migration Developer Guide 2026

key_points: Comprehensive license comparison: BSL vs MPL 2.0 and their practical implications
Feature-by-feature comparison including state encryption, dynamic variables, AI features
Migration path with backward compatibility emphasis
Decision framework: when to stay vs when to switch
State file compatibility and backup procedures
Real-world migration case study: 20 projects migrated
CI/CD pipeline migration examples for GitHub Actions and Jenkins

stats.fact: OpenTofu has approximately 12% adoption among IaC practitioners as of April 2026, with 27% of teams planning to evaluate or expand its use
Terraform commands 76% market share with 3,000+ providers and 12-year ecosystem
Infrastructure-as-Code market hit $2.1 billion in 2026 with 28.2% annual growth, driven by platform engineering adoption reaching 80% of large enterprises
OpenTofu has 140+ corporate backers and 13,000+ GitHub stars since its 2023 launch as a Linux Foundation project
HashiCorp changed Terraform's license from MPL to BSL in August 2023, triggering the OpenTofu fork
Enterprise adopters of OpenTofu include Boeing, Capital One, and AMD — primarily driven by license compliance and state encryption requirements
OpenTofu 1.9 delivered provider iteration with for_each and the -exclude flag, reducing code duplication in multi-region deployments
OpenTofu uses same state file format (JSON) as Terraform 1.5.x — migration requires no state conversion, just pointing the new binary at existing state

angles: The BSL license change: practical impact on teams building SaaS platforms, internal tools, and regulated-industry systems
OpenTofu's diverging feature set in 2026: native state encryption, dynamic backend variables, provider for_each — features Terraform still lacks
Proven step-by-step migration playbook: zero-downtime approach with incremental project migration and rollback paths
CI/CD modernization: replacing terraform commands with tofu in GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and GitLab CI
Decision framework: the matrix of when to migrate vs when to stay (license risk, feature needs, HCP dependency, team size)
Enterprise angle: regulated industries (finance, defense, government) choosing OpenTofu for open-source license compliance and air-gapped deployments
State file security: OpenTofu's native encryption vs Terraform's requirement for external workarounds
Community vs corporate governance: what OpenTofu's CNCF/Linux Foundation stewardship means for long-term stability
